behaviors.description: The embedding vector produced for a given query string has the correct dimensionality for the vector database index.
behaviors.justification: The behavior depends on an actual AI/ML embedding response shape, not just local wrapper logic. A stub can hide real model/version dimension mismatches. Integration is insufficient because correctness depends on real model output; Workflow is not required because this is a single model-output check, not a multi-agentic chain.
behaviors.test_description: Call the real embedding model for a fixed query and assert returned vector length exactly matches the live index dimension expected by the vector DB configuration.

behaviors.description: The similarity search returns results ordered by descending cosine similarity score.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily module handoff/ordering correctness between search service and vector-store access, and can be validated with real module wiring plus a local seeded store. Rejected System: behavior text does not require real HTTP/OS/subprocess or external API contract verification. Rejected Agentic: this step is about deterministic similarity ordering, not model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Seed deterministic vectors in a local test index, run similarity search through the service stack, and assert returned scores are monotonically non-increasing and IDs match expected order.

behaviors.description: The LLM re-ranking step changes the order of at least some results compared to the raw similarity ranking when the query has nuanced intent.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM ranking decisions for nuanced intent; mocked outputs would trivially pass and hide failures in actual relevance judgment. Rejected Workflow: only one agentic decision point (the reranker) is under test, not a multi-agentic sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run retrieval to get a fixed candidate set, invoke the real LLM reranker on nuanced queries, and assert at least one position differs from raw similarity ranking in repeated trials with acceptance thresholds.

behaviors.description: A query with no close matches in the database returns an empty result set rather than low-relevance noise.
behaviors.justification: The requirement is an externally visible API outcome (empty set) and should be proven via round-trip request/response, not only internal function calls. Rejected Agentic: behavior text is about no-match cutoff/output contract, not LLM output-quality judgment. Rejected Workflow: no multi-agentic sequence is required to validate this condition.
behaviors.test_description: Send real HTTP requests with out-of-domain queries against a running service and assert response payload is an empty results array (and expected status/shape), with readback from the API boundary.

behaviors.description: The end-to-end latency from receiving the query to returning re-ranked results stays below 2 seconds for the median case.
behaviors.justification: This is a full pipeline SLO across multiple sequential AI/ML steps (embedding + LLM rerank) plus retrieval/service orchestration. Unit/integration/system-only tests with stubs can hide real timing and model-service variability. Multi-step agentic execution is inherent in the stated end-to-end path.
behaviors.test_description: Execute production-like end-to-end requests through the running API with real embedding and reranking dependencies, collect latency distribution over a representative query set, and assert median p50 < 2.0s.
